summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an authenticated attacker can create a pass-through endpoint targeting an internal service and access it without proper authorization. The PoC demonstrates SSRF by creating a malicious endpoint and successfully fetching data from a simulated internal metadata service.
expected_security_impact: Creating a pass-through endpoint with a target URL pointing to an internal service should allow unauthorized access to that service. The PoC will demonstrate fetching sensitive data from a simulated AWS metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254) via the pass-through mechanism.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully started the LiteLLM proxy, created a pass-through endpoint targeting the simulated metadata service, and retrieved the secret data through the SSRF vulnerability. The attack required only a valid API key with no admin privileges.
verification_method: The reproduction script creates a simulated internal metadata service, configures a pass-through endpoint via the management API, and verifies that the endpoint successfully returns data from the internal service. The check distinguishes successful SSRF from proxy startup failures or configuration errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP GET request to the created endpoint successfully returned simulated metadata content.
No URL validation or authentication prevented the SSRF attack.
The proxy processed the request and forwarded it to the simulated internal service without blocking.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the LiteLLM repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
